name: Flaky Pie Dough
recipeIngredient: Cold unsalted butter (8 tbsp for one-crust, 16 tbsp for two-crust)
Cold water (2 tbsp for one-crust, 4 tbsp for two-crust)
recipeInstructions:
1. For the dough: In a medium-sized bowl, combine flour, salt, and baking powder. Stir well to mix.
2. Cut butter into 1 tablespoon pieces and add to dry ingredients. Toss once or twice to coat pieces of butter.
3. Break the butter into tiny pieces using your hands or a pastry blender. Pinch and squeeze it into the dry ingredients. Keep the mixture uniform by occasionally reaching down to the bottom of the bowl and mixing all the ingredients evenly together.
4. Continue rubbing the butter into the dry ingredients until the mixture resembles a coarse-ground cornmeal and no large pieces of butter remain visible.
5. Sprinkle the minimum amount of water over the butter and flour mixture and stir gently with a fork. The dough should begin holding together.
6. If the mixture still appears dry and crumbly, add the remaining water, 1 teaspoon at a time for the smaller quantity or one tablespoon at a time for the larger quantity, until the dough holds together easily.
7. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and form it into a disk (two equal disks for the larger amount of dough). Sandwich the disk(s) between two pieces of plastic wrap and press it into a 6-inch circle.
8. Refrigerate the dough until firm, or until you are ready to use it, at least 1 hour.
